evals.prompt: I need to set up a Tailscale policy for my startup. We have an engineering team (alice@startup.com, bob@startup.com) and one ops person (carl@startup.com). Engineers should be able to SSH into dev servers but NOT production. Carl should have full access everywhere. We also have CI/CD runners that need to deploy to staging. Write me a policy.hujson file for this.
evals.expected_output: A complete policy.hujson file with groups, tagOwners, ACL rules granting engineers access to dev (not prod), ops full access, CI access to staging, plus a tests block validating the key restrictions.

evals.prompt: I want to set up GitHub Actions so that whenever I push changes to my policy.hujson file, it automatically validates on PRs and deploys on merge to main. I have a Tailscale OAuth client ID and secret. Show me the complete GitHub Actions workflow YAML and what secrets I need to configure.
evals.expected_output: A complete .github/workflows/tailscale.yml with separate test (PR) and apply (push to main) steps using tailscale/gitops-acl-action@v1 with OAuth credentials, plus clear instructions on what secrets to add.

evals.prompt:
Can you explain the difference between ACLs and Grants in Tailscale policy files? And show me how to convert this ACL rule to use grants:

```json
{
  "action": "accept",
  "proto": "tcp",
  "src": ["group:backend"],
  "dst": ["tag:database:5432", "tag:database:5433"]
}
```
evals.expected_output: Clear explanation of ACLs vs Grants (syntax differences, when to use each), then the equivalent grants form with the port moved from dst to ip field.
